name: Performance Envelope
summary: A structured view of the speeds, altitudes, load factors, and mission conditions within which an aircraft can operate safely and effectively.
whenToUseIt: Use it when analysing aircraft capability, planning tests, or explaining why a design succeeds in one mission corner and fails in another.
steps: Identify key limits such as stall, thrust available, structural load factor, temperature, and altitude.
Plot the relevant boundaries for the mission or phase of flight.
Find the feasible operating region between those limits.
Check how payload, centre of gravity, or atmosphere shift the boundaries.
Use the result to make design or operational trade-offs.
example: A UAV may have excellent endurance at low altitude but insufficient climb margin at hot-and-high conditions. Mapping the performance envelope makes that gap obvious before flight testing begins.
